questions.question: What is Multilevel Feedback Queue Scheduling?
questions.answer: Multilevel Feedback Queue Scheduling allows processes to move between queues based on their behavior and execution history.

questions.question: What is denormalization?
questions.answer: Denormalization is the process of intentionally introducing redundancy to improve query performance.

questions.question: What is OSPF?
questions.answer: Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) is a link-state routing protocol that calculates shortest paths using Dijkstra's algorithm.

questions.question: What is the UPDATE statement?
questions.answer: UPDATE modifies existing records in a table.

questions.question: What is the difference between an iterable and an iterator?
questions.answer: An iterable can produce an iterator, whereas an iterator keeps track of iteration state and yields values one at a time.

questions.question: What is the map() function?
questions.answer: map() applies a function to every item in an iterable and returns an iterator.

questions.question: What is the difference between an interface and an abstract class?
questions.answer: An interface defines behavior contracts, while an abstract class can provide both contracts and partial implementation.

questions.question: What is contiguous memory allocation?
questions.answer: Contiguous memory allocation assigns a single continuous block of memory to a process.

questions.question: What is the ANY operator?
questions.answer: ANY compares a value to each value returned by a subquery and returns true if at least one comparison is true.

questions.question: What is TCP congestion control?
questions.answer: TCP congestion control adjusts the rate of data transmission based on network conditions to avoid congestion.

questions.question: What is data independence?
questions.answer: Data independence is the ability to modify the database schema without affecting application programs.

questions.question: What is the CAP Theorem?
questions.answer: The CAP Theorem states that a distributed system can guarantee at most two of Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance simultaneously.

questions.question: When should NoSQL databases be preferred?
questions.answer: NoSQL databases are preferred for large-scale distributed systems requiring flexibility, scalability, and high throughput.

questions.question: What is the advantage of Bellman-Ford over Dijkstra?
questions.answer: Bellman-Ford can detect negative weight cycles and works with negative edge weights.

questions.question: Why are Red-Black Trees preferred in many standard libraries?
questions.answer: They provide guaranteed O(log n) operations while requiring fewer rotations than AVL trees.

questions.question: What is an interface?
questions.answer: An interface defines a contract of methods that implementing classes must provide.
